nodes.context: A gang and music group founded in the South Bronx in the late 1960s, involved in politics and the Puerto Rican independence movement.
A political organization involved in the Puerto Rican independence movement, similar to the Ghetto Brothers.
A political party associated with the Ghetto Brothers and the Puerto Rican independence movement.
The female auxiliary of the Ghetto Brothers, treated as organization members and girlfriends.
A gang that merged with the Ghetto Brothers in the Connecticut prison system to form Los Solidos.
A powerful Puerto Rican gang in Connecticut formed from the merger of the Ghetto Brothers and Savage Nomads.

edges.context: Both were involved in politics and the Puerto Rican independence movement.
The Ghetto Brothers were associated with the Puerto Rican Socialist Party.
The Ghetto Sisters were the female auxiliary of the Ghetto Brothers.
In the Connecticut prison system during the 1990s, the Ghetto Brothers and Savage Nomads joined to form Los Solidos.
The Ghetto Brothers merged with Savage Nomads to form Los Solidos.
The Savage Nomads merged with Ghetto Brothers to form Los Solidos.
